description: ol-style-name-case (issue #115): a `return` statement written non-lowercase (`RETURN`) is flagged the same way a non-lowercase control keyword is. `ReturnNode.keyword` itself only ever stores the canonical `"return"` spelling the parser normalizes to (never the literal source casing), so this check works by slicing `source` at the node's own span start, exactly like the control-keyword case.
